name: Alternative Choice Close
summary: A closing technique that offers two credible forward paths so the buyer chooses how to proceed rather than whether to proceed at all.
whenToUseIt: Use the alternative choice close when the buyer is already leaning in and needs help choosing the next commitment, timeline, or rollout shape.
steps: Identify two realistic next-step options that both move the deal forward.
Present the options clearly without sounding manipulative.
Use the choice to clarify the buyer's preferred path and timing.
example: The AE asks, 'Would it make more sense to run the security review this week and procurement next week, or bring both workstreams together in one executive review?'
